device.name: Hydronic Fan-Coil Unit (with air filter)
device.identified_from: visual + on-site confirmation: water supply pipe, bottom access panels, air filter + grille (air drawn through -> has a blower), single cable powering the fan. Industrial building with central boiler.

heat_source: Building hot-water boiler (gas/central plant) for heat; electricity powers only the blower fan

electricity_in_use_w.note: blower fan when running; PSC vs ECM motor affects the figure

standby_plugged_in_w.note: controls only when fan is off

estimated_annual_electricity_kwh.note: fan runtime dependent; only the fan, not the heat

power_consumption.thermal_note: Heating/cooling energy comes from boiler/chiller water (BTU/hr), not electricity. Only the fan is on the electric bill.

notes: CONFIRMED HYDRONIC FAN-COIL. The filter + grille means a fan draws room air across a hot-water coil; the single cable is the fan power. Electric load is just the blower (~50-150 W running); the actual heat is boiler fuel.
